summary: postinstall pipes unpinned remote shell script to sh from third-party domain
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js (lines 82-83) executes `npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh`. The fallback fetches a shell script from opencode.ai over HTTPS and pipes it directly into `sh` with no version pin, no commit pin, and no hash/signature verification. opencode.ai is not the publisher's own domain, so any compromise, takeover, or MITM of that endpoint yields arbitrary code execution on every machine that installs openprompt-lang (developer workstations and CI runners alike). The same block also globally installs an unrelated third-party CLI (`@opencode/cli`) without consent, broadening the installer's trust footprint. The package's advertised purpose (a prompt/annotation language) does not require pulling a third-party AI coding tool at install time, and even if it did, the canonical install flow is to declare a dependency rather than curl-pipe-to-sh from an unrelated domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
